title: Paleolithic life
description: Test your knowledge of paleolithic life!
topic_path: The origin of humans and early human societies

assessment_items.question_content:
Paleolithic (“Stone Age”) refers to the fact that during this period humans: 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Began using stone tools to manipulate their environments
Painted on the stone walls of caves
Used stone exclusively as a building material

assessment_items.question_content:
One reason for increased human cooperation and group organization during the Paleolithic was that:


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allowed for division of labor and specialization to better exploit resources
Agriculture was only possible with organized groups

assessment_items.question_content:
Evidence for the widespread use of language by humans during the Paleolithic includes: 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Long-distance travel and trade, and the organization of complex social and cultural structures
Various written inscriptions found at Paleolithic sites
The ability to hunt animals for food

assessment_items.question_content:
What can the discovery of musical instruments and cave paintings tell us about Paleolithic humans? 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The specific nature of their cultural practices and beliefs
That they had cultural practices and beliefs, but not specific details

assessment_items.question_content:
Agriculture had an advantage over hunting and foraging because it:



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Required less labor than hunting and foraging
Improved the regularity and efficiency of food supplies
